Download for iOS Go to your assistant The most important goals in life are those no-one can do for you. Learning a new skill, building financial security, moving to a new country, becoming a better friend. I can't do that. But I was created to be the best companion for everything that matters. I am here to help you define what you want, make a plan, and show up every day to see it through. I'll research what you need to know and break down big ambitions into Tuesday morning tasks. And I'm just as ready to capture a fleeting thought and answer any question. Life is not just about grand ambitions, and I'm here to be your partner in (metaphorical) crime. Goals New home for your ambitions. For each goal, I create a visual timeline of your progress , collect related notes , and handle reminders to help you with your plan. Track day-to-day progress towards your goal. Your assistant does research and updates your goals whenever it's needed. Home view with your goals, notes and chat. Notes Thoughts need space. Use notes to organize your work and fun , and I'll join in. I can read along to understand your context, and write or edit notes whenever you need me to. Focused editing experience for your notes. items.description: The fastest way to mute yourself on video calls

website.title: CapslockMute - Mute Zoom, Tandem, Meet with Caps Lock
headings: Your data stays on your Mac
website.text_excerpt: CapslockMute - Mute Zoom, Tandem, Meet with Caps Lock 🎙️ CapslockMute Press Caps Lock to mute yourself in Tandem Zoom Meet Download for macOS v1.3 · macOS 10.15+ "Caps Lock → Mute is the life hack you didn't know you needed." — Rajiv ⚡ Instant Mute Press Caps Lock to toggle mute. No hunting for buttons. 🔒 Secure Signed and notarized by Apple. No Gatekeeper warnings. 🪶 Lightweight Runs silently in the background. No CPU impact. Installation 1 Download and unzip Download the zip file above and double-click to extract it. 2 Move to Applications Drag CapslockMute.app to your Applications folder. 3 Grant Accessibility permission On first launch, macOS will ask for Accessibility permission. This is required to detect your Caps Lock key. 4 Select your meeting software Choose from Zoom, Tandem , or Meet in the menu bar settings. Requirements macOS 10.15 (Catalina) or later No Karabiner-Elements (conflicts with key remapping) "Mute should be muscle memory." — Rajiv 🤫 About to sneeze? Hit Caps Lock to mute. Want to dictate a note via Aqua Voice while on a call? Hit Caps Lock to mute. Child throwing a tantrum? Hit Caps Lock to mute. Loud car horns near your house? Hit Caps Lock to mute. Privacy Your data stays on your Mac CapslockMute doesn't collect any data. It doesn't connect to the internet. It just listens for Caps Lock and sends a mute shortcut to Tandem/Zoom/Meet. That's it. View on GitHub · MIT License

website.text_excerpt: StoryCV | Resume Bullet Points That Get Interviews. Finally know what to say on your resume. Great resume bullet points start with reflection, not writing. Talk through your work with StoryCV and the bullets write themselves. Write my resume See how it works → Product Manager Worked closely with engineering and design teams to define and ship product features. Product Manager Pushed through a checkout redesign that three previous PMs had deprioritised, shipped it in 6 weeks, and cut cart abandonment by 18%. Before After StoryCV As featured on Bullet points are the hardest part. You've worked across projects, teams and changing goals. Your impact lies in the decisions and trade-offs. But your resume asks you to: Squeeze years of work into one-line bullet points Figure out which achievements actually matter Translate company jargon to something everyone understands So you rewrite. Then rewrite again. And somehow, it still doesn't feel right. The best writing always starts with the best research. Most resumes skip the conversation. So we flipped the order . Start resume writing with a conversation. If you sat down for coffee with a world-class resume writer, you wouldn't hand them a list of keywords. You’d tell them stories and explain the context. 1 2 3 1 Have a guided conversation about your work No need to overthink. Talk through your work without worrying about wording or length. 2 We extract the impact from your story We handle the editorial thinking: deciding what matters, what to prioritize, and what to cut. 3 Get ready-to-use bullet points Clear, concise bullets you can use across your resume and applications.
